title: senecio haloragis var. glandulosus
intro: Senecio haloragis var. glandulosus, also known as a variety of the Woolly Groundsel, is a plant species native to Australia and New Zealand. It is used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relieve digestive issues, reduce pain, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Senecio haloragis var. glandulosus is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing coughs, and promoting relaxation.
